title: Series and Death
wikitext:
[]: Series and Death             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Mon Dec 11, 2006 9:58 pm Is there anything in the series that is not explained in Death that a person should know/see before they see EoE? I was thinking of showing some of the DC eps, or just scenes that would help create the environment for the movie (Asuka's bathtub scene, mindrape, 21', etc) thanks

[]: Re: Series and Death             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Dec 11, 2006 11:20 pm InvaderRen wrote: Is there anything in the series that is not explained in Death that a person should know/see before they see EoE? I was thinking of showing some of the DC eps, or just scenes that would help create the environment for the movie (Asuka's bathtub scene, mindrape, 21', etc) thanks

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Dec 12, 2006 12:14 am Death is NOT a crash course for Evangelion. Death is for when you want to get the full experience from EoE but don't have the time to rewatch the TV Series beforehand.

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Tue Dec 12, 2006 1:06 pm I agree with Monkey and Coleman. Death wouldn't make any sense to someone who hasen't seen most or all of the series. It's meant to be a refresher to those who've seen EVA already, not replace it altogether.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Dec 12, 2006 6:56 pm Death was a stop-gap solution for Gainix to make some money out of the half completed EoE, and get Anno some cinematics hsots for #26'.

[]: Re: Series and Death             Quote Post by Rawinder » Tue Dec 12, 2006 8:28 pm Ornette wrote: As far as recaps go, I thought Death was refreshingly well executed. The way bits and pieces of the series were stringed together in an almost coherent statement

[]: Re: Series and Death             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Dec 12, 2006 9:10 pm Rawinder wrote: I have to disagree. Death was anything but coherent. The pacing of the "movie" is essentially a stream of consciousness that jumps back and forth between time periods and characters.

[]: Re: Series and Death             Quote Post by Rawinder » Wed Dec 13, 2006 6:51 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Rawinder wrote: I have to disagree. Death was anything but coherent. The pacing of the "movie" is essentially a stream of consciousness that jumps back and forth between time periods and characters.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Dec 14, 2006 5:25 am By "almost coherent statement", I meant that it was trying to say something that was different than what you got from the series. It pieced the series together in such a way (of course, it payed no mind to the timeline) that it was almost telling you something new. And for that reason is why Death is not a substitute for the series itself. That was all I was trying to say by my post. I used the fact that I really enjoyed it and thought it was well done as far as a recap goes as a contrast to that fact.

[]:             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Thu Dec 14, 2006 4:07 pm hmm i see what you people mean most of the ppl coming i have coming to watch the movie have seen the series, there is just 2-3 that either dont have the time or wouldnt put up with seeing a 26 ep. series for my sake, so im going to HAVE to use Death somewhat. But i fully understand what you guys mean. Still, any advice?
